title: Audit Storage Capacity and Index Growth Guardrails

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 AU-4 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 AU-4 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.14.3: Audit Storage Capacity and Index Growth Guardrails.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.3' running on _internal (metrics.log license), /services/data/indexes (index size, frozenTimePeriodInSecs), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (REST, DMC)
spl:
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 NOT title IN ("_*")
| eval size_gb=currentDBSizeMB/1024
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,1)
| table title, size_gb, maxTotalDataSizeMB, retention_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs
| sort - size_gb
description: Detects indexes approaching disk or license limits that could cause loss of audit evidence, supporting AU-4 audit storage capacity management.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Run from a scheduled search with `rest` rights; (2) compare `size_gb` to business thresholds by index class; (3) integrate license usage from `_internal` for combined risk scoring; (4) open change requests before forced rolling.
visualization: Single value (indexes over threshold), Table (top consumers), Column chart (size by index). Key fields: `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `maxTotalDataSizeMB`, `retention_days`, `size_gb`, `title`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for detects indexes approaching disk or license limits that could cause loss of audit evidence, supporting audit storage capacity management. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (REST, DMC).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `_internal` (metrics.log license), `/services/data/indexes` (index size, frozenTimePeriodInSecs).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Run from a scheduled search with `rest` rights; (2) compare `size_gb` to business thresholds by index class; (3) integrate license usage from `_internal` for combined risk scoring; (4) open change requests before forced rolling.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 NOT title IN ("_*")
| eval size_gb=currentDBSizeMB/1024
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,1)
| table title, size_gb, maxTotalDataSizeMB, retention_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs
| sort - size_gb
```

Understanding this SPL

**Audit Storage Capacity and Index Growth Guardrails (AU-4)** ‚Äî Detects indexes approaching disk or license limits that could cause loss of audit evidence, supporting AU-4 audit storage capacity management.

Documented **Data sources**: `_internal` (metrics.log license), `/services/data/indexes` (index size, frozenTimePeriodInSecs). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (REST, DMC). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **size_gb** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **retention_days** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, retention_days, size_gb, title.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (indexes over threshold), Table (top consumers), Column chart (size by index)
